stages.name: unit_tests_failing_first
stages.evidence: Before implementation, task-specific tests exposed the audited regressions: explicit --config cwd-relative reads, generated same-file command parsing for metacharacter paths, doctest AI snapshot refs and project metadata leaking into prompts, malformed AI reports fabricating command evidence, doctest mutation parse failures disappearing, cleanup-warning visibility, and overclaimed coverage-gap rows.

stages.name: unit_tests
stages.evidence: Full suite passed after implementation (544 tests).

stages.name: task_system_validation_active
stages.evidence: Validator passed with task 124 active after implementation and before completion-state edits.

stages.name: task_system_validation_complete
stages.evidence: Validator passed after moving task 124 to complete with active_task and next_task set to null.

residual_risk: F-045 coverage is intentionally not overstated: shared symlink containment and explicit --config adapter behavior are executable, while separate symlink fixtures for every guarded CLI read/write call site remain out of this task's claim.
Generated same-file command evidence still stores rendered command text for reports; execution paths use structured argv specs so report text is not reparsed for mutation classification.
AI contexts reject malformed missing command evidence rather than inventing defaults; this can make previously accepted malformed fixtures fail as invalid reports, which is the intended contract.
